sources.title: AI Reasoning Models 2026: From OpenAI o3 to DeepSeek-R1 and the Test-Time Compute Revolution
claims: Reasoning models trained via reinforcement learning to 'think' before responding, spending additional compute at inference time
o3 released April 2025 (GA); priced at $2/$8 per M tokens after June 2025 price cut
As of February 2026, o3 is the strongest reasoning model with ARC-AGI score of 96.7%

sources.title: Global VC investment surges to record $330.9 billion in Q1'26 on back of AI megadeals
claims: Global venture capital investment more than doubled from Q4'25 to record $330.9B in Q1'26
AI captured 80% of total global venture funding in Q1 2026 ($242B)
Four of the five largest venture rounds ever recorded closed in Q1 2026 (OpenAI $122B, Anthropic $30B, xAI $20B, Waymo $16B)

sources.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
claims: DeepSeek-R1 released January 20, 2025 with Apache-2.0 open weights
Achieves performance comparable to OpenAI o1 on reasoning benchmarks
Distilled variants (7B–70B) make frontier reasoning accessible at low cost

sources.title: AI Inference Market Size, Share & Growth, 2025 To 2030
claims: Inference-optimized chip market to exceed $50B in 2026
Inference workloads will account for roughly two-thirds of all AI compute in 2026 (up from one-third in 2023)
Market growth driven by test-time compute, generative AI, and reasoning workloads

sources.title: China's open source AI models to capture a larger share of 2026 global AI market
claims: DeepSeek and Qwen grew from combined 1% of global AI market share (Jan 2025) to roughly 15% (Jan 2026)
Described as 'the fastest adoption curve in AI history'
OpenAI retains 40% global share; DeepSeek now at 6%, Qwen at 9%

sources.title: The 2026 Legal AI Reckoning: A Case-by-Case Breakdown of Every Major Hallucination Incident
claims: Multiple AI hallucination incidents in US courts during 2026
Federal courts now mandate disclosure of AI use in legal filings
Hallucinated citations led to disciplinary proceedings and malpractice exposure

sources.title: DeepSeek R1 vs OpenAI o3 vs Gemini 3: Reasoning Model Benchmarks 2026
claims: Anthropic commands 40% of enterprise LLM spending, ahead of OpenAI (27%) and Google (21%)
85% of Anthropic revenue comes from business customers
Gemini 3.1 Pro tops GPQA Diamond reasoning benchmark at 94.3%
Claude Sonnet 4.6 leads GDPval-AA Elo benchmark with 1,633 points

sources.title: Where AI is Headed in 2026
claims: The market has shifted primary expenditure from model training to large-scale deployment — 'Inference Economics'
Inference workloads account for two-thirds of all compute in 2026
Post-training scaling and test-time scaling suggest world needs significantly more data centers

sources.title: 85 Predictions for AI and the Law in 2026
claims: Over 35 state bar associations have issued guidance requiring attorneys to verify AI-generated content
Multiple federal courts now mandate disclosure of AI use in filings
Regulators will start requiring provable transparency and audit trails for AI tools

sources.title: State of AI 2026 — AI Market Size, Investment, and Industry Data
claims: Total global AI spending expected to exceed $2.02T in 2026 (+36% annual increase)
78% enterprise adoption of AI by 2026
67% of enterprises now deploy open-weight models in production (up from 23% in 2024)
Open source AI market has grown 340% year-over-year in 2026

sources.title: AI Reasoning Models in 2026: How o1-Like Advances Are Reshaping Enterprise Problem-Solving
claims: Enterprises deploying AI for code generation report 40% developer productivity improvements
Reasoning models handle multi-file, multi-step coding tasks beyond autocomplete limitations
IDC Taiwan: enterprise investment in reasoning models to grow 300%+ in 2026 vs 2025

sources.title: DeepSeek-R1 — AI Wiki
claims: DeepSeek-R1 released January 20, 2025 — open weights, Apache license
DeepSeek-R1-0528 update released May 28, 2025 with improved reasoning depth and benchmark performance
Made strong reasoning performance available via open model weights, web access, and API access

unverified_claims.claim: search_volume: 90K+/mo — estimated from awareness data and query trend analysis; no direct verified keyword tool data available
growth_3m: +87% — estimated proxy based on Anthropic ARR trajectory (Q3 2025 ~$10B → Q1 2026 ~$19B); enterprise reasoning model investment surge in Q1 2026
Meta market share: 4% — estimated; Meta recently launched new model after $14B Alexandr Wang deal; exact reasoning model share unconfirmed
xAI Grok 4.2 benchmark claims vs Gemini on MMMU and MATH — per aggregated benchmark sites; pending independent replication
